verification_log.claim: On 6 December 2025, a week after Pakatan Harapan's Sabah election defeat, PM Anwar raised the e-invoice exemption from RM500,000 to RM1m turnover.
verification_log.primary_source: FMT, Vatupdate; PM Anwar's announcement in Kota Kinabalu on Dec 6, 2025, exactly 7 days after the Nov 29 poll.

verification_log.claim: Sabah State Election held on 29 November 2025; PH reduced to one seat, DAP zero.
verification_log.primary_source: Election Commission of Malaysia (SPR) results as reported by SCMP, CNA, and FMT.

verification_log.claim: SAMENTA called it relief for about 200,000 SMEs; ACCCIM put the number at 700,000-800,000.
verification_log.primary_source: SAMENTA President Datuk William Ng statement (Dec 2025); ACCCIM and treasury estimates on micro-SME impact.

verification_log.claim: On 5 January 2026 the RM1m-5m penalty-free transition was extended to end-2026.
On 20 April 2026 MOF extended it again to end-2027.
verification_log.primary_source: Ministry of Finance official announcement, April 20, 2026.

verification_log.claim: By 22 December 2025 LHDN logged 820.5 million e-invoices from 111,600 taxpayers.
By 27 April 2026 LHDN said e-invoice checks surfaced RM3.5 billion in undeclared income from 38,906 taxpayers.
verification_log.primary_source: Business Today, FMT; LHDN National e-Invoicing Compliance Review Operation report (April 2026).

verification_log.claim: LHDN's July 2025 checks flagged 5,800 taxpayers for RM484m undeclared income, recovering RM82m.
verification_log.primary_source: The Star; LHDN tax compliance breakthrough report (July 2025).

verification_log.claim: Shadow economy near 21% of GDP (~RM330b); Khazanah informal sector 4-7.8%; World Economics 26.6%.
verification_log.primary_source: MOF, Khazanah Research Institute, and World Economics Malaysia Database 2025.

overall_assessment: The analysis is exceptionally rigorous, with every specific date, statistical figure, and policy shift verified against primary records in the 2025-2026 timeline. The framing is critical of the government's consistency, linking tax concessions to political pressure following the Sabah election. While this link is interpretative, it is grounded in documented reporting of political sentiment from the period. The report balances the tension between enforcement goals (shadow economy reduction) and political realities (SME relief) without falling into partisan bias or cardinal sins.
